title: Want to own a piece of Dunkin’? Its parent company may go public.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Inspire Brands plans to go public, making it the third time Dunkin' has gone public in eight decades.
The company filed confidentially with the SEC for an IPO, with no set date, price, or ticker symbol announced.
Funds from the IPO will help Inspire Brands pay down debt, improve credit terms, and increase brand awareness.

title: Trooper who helped take down Memorial Drive gunman identified as victims’ families speak out
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Trooper Landon Veney praised for heroism in taking down gunman Tyler Brown after chaotic Memorial Drive shooting.
Tyler Brown faces charges including armed assault with intent to murder; he was shot and awaits arraignment.
Victims include a Somerville father and DoorDash driver, both injured but expected to survive, families express gratitude.

title: A new grocery store is coming to the Seaport
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Whole Foods Market plans to open a Daily Shop at 111 Harbor Way in Boston's Seaport.
The Daily Shop will offer select Whole Foods items for quick shopping in dense urban areas.
Whole Foods has yet to announce an opening date for the Seaport location but plans more in Chicago and Philadelphia.

title: Travelers can now get real-time security checkpoint wait times at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport introduces real-time security checkpoint wait times on its website and FlyLogan app.
The digital tool uses cameras to monitor line length without recording personal details, enhancing travel planning.
Massport's effort improves customer experience, contributing to Logan's high ranking in airport satisfaction studies.

title: MBTA video shows man throwing ‘temper tantrum’ and blocking traffic after missing bus
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man blocked an MBTA bus on Mt. Auburn Street in Cambridge on May 6 after missing it.
The man is facing charges for blocking the bus and causing traffic disruption, according to transit police.
A 33-year-old passenger with warrants allegedly threatened the man with a knife and is also facing charges.

title: Mansfield becomes first town in Mass. to pass near-total ban on data centers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Mansfield passes zoning bylaw limiting data center operations, becoming the first in Massachusetts to do so.
New rules allow only Tier I data centers in specific districts, with a focus on preserving infrastructure capacity.
The bylaw imposes noise restrictions and requires detailed compliance reports for data center permit applicants.

title: Spate of Boston gun violence continues with Dorchester shooting Tuesday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A shooting in Dorchester early Tuesday left one person in critical condition, marking Boston's fifth shooting in a week.
The incident near 25 Dacia St. caused ballistic damage to structures and vehicles; no arrests have been made.
Boston has recorded two fatal shootings this year, down from six in the same period last year.

title: 8 New England beach towns are among the best on the East Coast, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Eight New England beach towns featured in Conde Nast Traveler's best on the East Coast list.
Massachusetts' Oak Bluffs, Nantucket, and Rockport named among top East Coast beach towns.
Newport, R.I., Bar Harbor, Maine, and Hampton Beach, N.H. also highlighted in the travel list.

title: His truck was stolen from a Logan Airport garage. Massport initially refused a parking refund.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes's truck was stolen from Logan Airport's garage, and he initially faced a $276 parking fee with no refund.
Massport initially denied Byrnes's refund request, citing vague 'circumstances,' but later agreed to process the refund after media inquiries.
Byrnes remains skeptical about the refund and has accepted the loss of his vehicle, believing it to be irretrievable.

title: Patriots rookie Quintayvious Hutchins pleads not guilty to assault and battery charge
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots rookie Quintayvious Hutchins pleaded not guilty to a misdemeanor assault and battery charge in Newton District Court.
The incident occurred at a Boston College residence hall; Hutchins allegedly grabbed a woman by the neck during a dispute.
Hutchins, a Boston College alumnus, was released on personal recognizance with a pretrial hearing set for June 29.
